title: Support Escalation Evidence Investigator
business_outcome: reduce time to an evidence-complete escalation handoff while preserving uncertainty and human control of customer or repository actions
llm_justification: Ticket narratives and repository evidence use inconsistent language and can conflict. A model can produce a bounded synthesis and proposed follow-up, while deterministic code verifies evidence identifiers, policy, state transitions, and authority.

workflow_steps.name: Accept escalation job
workflow_steps.purpose: Validate the request, establish correlation, and create durable state.
workflow_steps.output_contract: EscalationJob row and immutable input reference
workflow_steps.failure_behavior: Reject invalid requests without starting the graph.

workflow_steps.name: Gather bounded evidence
workflow_steps.purpose: Read the ticket, linked GitHub records, deployment state, and status incidents.
workflow_steps.output_contract: Typed evidence records with source IDs and observed timestamps
workflow_steps.failure_behavior: Classify timeout, permission, rate limit, not found, and partial evidence.

workflow_steps.name: Synthesize investigation
workflow_steps.purpose: Reconcile narrative symptoms and conflicting evidence into bounded findings.
workflow_steps.output_contract: Strict InvestigationDraft with evidence IDs, uncertainty, and proposed actions
workflow_steps.failure_behavior: Classify provider or schema failure and preserve retry-safe graph state.

workflow_steps.name: Validate evidence and policy
workflow_steps.purpose: Reject unknown evidence IDs, unsupported claims, forbidden actions, and stale source versions.
workflow_steps.output_contract: Validated investigation or explicit validation findings
workflow_steps.failure_behavior: Route to needs-review without executing any external action.

workflow_steps.name: Review consequential follow-up
workflow_steps.purpose: Allow a support lead to approve, edit, reject, or expire proposed writes.
workflow_steps.output_contract: Durable decision bound to proposal and evidence version
workflow_steps.failure_behavior: Remain interrupted; stale or invalid decisions are rejected.

workflow_steps.name: Execute approved follow-up
workflow_steps.purpose: Post only the approved ticket note or GitHub issue with idempotent reconciliation.
workflow_steps.output_contract: External receipt and durable execution record
workflow_steps.failure_behavior: Reconcile uncertain writes before retry and surface unresolved state.

integrations.name: GitHub REST
integrations.purpose: Read linked issues and pull requests and create an approved follow-up issue.
integrations.fixture_strategy: Versioned repository, issue, pull request, pagination, rate-limit, and write-reconciliation fixtures.

integrations.name: Support Ticket API
integrations.purpose: Read escalation history and post an approved evidence-linked internal note.
integrations.fixture_strategy: Versioned ticket histories, status transitions, unsafe text, and idempotency fixtures.

integrations.name: Service Status API
integrations.purpose: Read component state and incident timelines that may confirm or contradict the ticket.
integrations.fixture_strategy: Versioned component and incident snapshots with delayed and missing updates.

model_boundary.configuration_policy: Model identifier, timeout, retry budget, and reasoning settings are environment configuration; capability and evaluation evidence control changes.

orchestration.justification: The workflow needs typed state, fan-in after multiple evidence tools, checkpointed provider retries, a durable human interrupt, source-version validation on resume, and replay-safe recovery after uncertain external writes.

state_choices.name: PostgreSQL
state_choices.purpose: Durable jobs, evidence versions, graph checkpoints, proposals, decisions, and execution receipts.
state_choices.loss_behavior: The workflow cannot safely continue; restore from backup and reconcile external receipts.

state_choices.name: Redis
state_choices.purpose: Transient progress events, short-lived deduplication hints, and bounded rate-limit coordination.
state_choices.loss_behavior: Progress subscriptions and hints are lost, but durable jobs and replay safety remain correct.

approval_points.action: Post a support note or create a GitHub follow-up issue
approval_points.enforcement_boundary: A FastAPI administrative route outside the model-callable tool surface persists a decision bound to proposal and evidence hashes.
approval_points.replay_strategy: Execution checks proposal status, expiry, source hashes, idempotency key, and existing external marker before writing.

evaluation.ground_truth_source: Synthetic cases labeled from ticket, repository, deployment, and status evidence using a mentor-approved policy with adjudication.
evaluation.holdout_policy: Freeze 12 untouched blind mentor cases before baseline implementation; development cases and holdout IDs remain separate.
evaluation.baseline: Deterministic keyword and link-following workflow with no model synthesis.

delivery.docker_smoke_test: Start API, worker, PostgreSQL, and Redis; submit a fixture escalation; approve a proposal out of band; verify one reconciled write and result.

milestones.name: walking skeleton
milestones.demo: One fixture escalation reaches a validated investigation result.

milestones.name: evidence boundaries
milestones.demo: GitHub, ticket, and status failures remain classified and source-linked.

milestones.name: durability and approval
milestones.demo: Restart, approve out of band, execute once, and replay safely.

milestones.name: evaluation iterations
milestones.demo: Compare baseline and two targeted interventions by slice.

milestones.name: delivery and defense
milestones.demo: Run clean install, Docker smoke, red team, and five-minute walkthrough.

redis.rationale: Transient progress and coordination improve the asynchronous API without becoming durable truth.

fastmcp.rationale: No identified external MCP client needs this investigation capability; FastAPI is the supported product interface.

risks.description: Untrusted ticket or GitHub text attempts to expand authority.
risks.trigger: External text contains tool or approval instructions.
risks.mitigation: Treat content as quoted evidence, use fixed tools, deterministic policy, and out-of-band approval.

risks.description: Valid structured output cites evidence that does not exist.
risks.trigger: Draft contains an unknown or stale evidence identifier.
risks.mitigation: Deterministically verify every evidence ID and source hash before approval.

risks.description: Crash after external write creates duplicate follow-up.
risks.trigger: External API succeeds before local receipt commits.
risks.mitigation: Use stable idempotency keys or markers and reconcile before retry.

risks.description: Evaluation labels reward plausible prose instead of workflow correctness.
risks.trigger: High narrative score coexists with missing evidence or invalid actions.
risks.mitigation: Score evidence, policy, and workflow dimensions separately with deterministic checks.

risks.description: Integration scope prevents a polished delivery.
risks.trigger: Walking skeleton misses the first milestone review.
risks.mitigation: Freeze optional UI and MCP work and retain fixture-backed vertical scope.

risks.description: Sensitive ticket data enters prompts or logs.
risks.trigger: Fixtures or traces contain secret-shaped or unnecessary customer fields.
risks.mitigation: Minimize fields, redact before provider and logging boundaries, and test redaction.
